CONDITIONAL. Foundry Yard passes the core-plus going-in debt screens on actual deal terms: 60.0% LTV, 1.29x amortizing DSCR, and 9.41% debt yield, with no hard threshold dealbreaker. The condition is rent-roll quality and concentration: the OM says ~60% of residents work for Carolina Logistics, while the rent roll lacks tenant/employer and lease-expiration detail needed to quantify correlated vacancy risk. Revenue also needs reconciliation because current rent roll billing annualizes materially above T-12 EGI.

CONDITIONAL. The rent roll itself is acceptable: all 120 units are accounted for, physical occupancy is 94.17%, and loss-to-lease is modest at 3.21% of occupied market rent. The condition is not a rent-roll dealbreaker; it is that current scheduled rent does not reconcile cleanly to T12 EGI, and the offering memo/T12 flag an understated insurance line that could compress NOI. At the deal-file 6.00% amortizing debt rate, DSCR is 1.23x, above the core-plus 1.20x strategy minimum but below the general 1.25x pass threshold, so this is fixable through revenue reconciliation, corrected insurance, and final debt sizing.

perDealbreaker.matchedText: CONDITIONAL. The rent roll supports an opportunistic turnaround thesis, but not a clean proceed: current occupancy is only 70.83%, going-in amortizing DSCR is 0.57x at the proposed 75% LTV, and the identified loss-to-lease is moderate rather than enough by itself to fix coverage. This is not a rent-roll PASS until the team verifies a funded lease-up / bridge structure and reconciles the rent roll to actual collections.

On current NOI, going-in amortizing DSCR is only 0.10x, so the rent-roll upside is not enough to make this proceedable as structured.

perFlag.matchedText: CONDITIONAL. The rent roll supports a real value-add thesis, but not an unconditional pass: 89.84% occupancy is just under the 90% acceptable occupancy threshold, going-in amortizing DSCR is 1.15x against the 1.25x pass threshold, and debt yield is 8.53% against the 9.0% pass threshold. This is not a dealbreaker because LTV is clean at 70%, DSCR is above the 1.10x conditional band and far above the 0.80 dealbreaker level, and pro forma NOI would support a 1.44x amortizing DSCR if lease-up/rent capture is verified. Missing snapshot/source artifacts lower confidence, but the verdict is conditional because current coverage and revenue validation are marginal.

CONDITIONAL. The rent roll supports a real value-add thesis, but not an unconditional pass: 89.84% occupancy is just under the 90% acceptable occupancy threshold, going-in amortizing DSCR is 1.15x against the 1.25x pass threshold, and debt yield is 8.53% against the 9.0% pass threshold. This is not a dealbreaker because LTV is clean at 70%, DSCR is above the 1.10x conditional band and far above the 0.80 dealbreaker level, and pro forma NOI would support a 1.44x amortizing DSCR if lease-up/rent capture is verified. Missing snapshot/source artifacts lower confidence, but the verdict is conditional because current coverage and revenue validation are marginal.

CONDITIONAL — Crestline Park has a real but moderate rent-roll value-add case: 91.03% physical occupancy, $204,480 annual occupied-unit loss-to-lease, and pro forma NOI rising to $1.56M. The deal is not a FAIL under the stated rule because it has a clear value-add thesis and going-in amortizing DSCR is above the 0.80 dealbreaker line, but the 82% LTV, 0.85x amortizing DSCR, 0.99x IO DSCR, and weak EGI capture require a financing recut and rent-roll/T12 reconciliation before proceeding.

CONDITIONAL. Brookhaven Flats clears the hard rent-roll thresholds: 87.33% physical occupancy is above the 70% dealbreaker line, 72.0% LTV is within pass range, and going-in amortizing DSCR is 1.13x, which is thin but above the value-add minimum and not a fail. The deal depends on proving the 19-unit lease-up, reconciling T-12 EGI to current scheduled rent, and validating market rents; absent those, the rent-roll thesis is plausible but not clean enough for PASS.
